source_pr.title: txn: Add test case to cover the case that check_txn_status on primary lock of primary txn may affect transaction correctness
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #51666

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

This PR adds a test to ensure that #51666 (https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/16620) is fixed.

Requires:
* https://github.com/tikv/client-go/pull/1211
* https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/16621

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: tk2.MustQuery("select * from t order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("1 11", "2 21")) (tests/realtikvtest/txntest/txn_test.go)
failure_signature: tk2.MustQuery("select * from t order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("1 11", "2 21")) (tests/realtikvtest/txntest/txn_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: tk2.MustQuery("select * from t order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("1 11", "2 21")) (tests/realtikvtest/txntest/txn_test.go)

analysis:
PR title: txn: Add test case to cover the case that check_txn_status on primary lock of primary txn may affect transaction correctness
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/51667
Triage sources: manual_triage, determinism_stabilization
Triage summary: tk2.MustQuery("select * from t order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("1 11", "2 21")) (tests/realtikvtest/txntest/txn_test.go)
Changed files (2): tests/realtikvtest/txntest/BUILD.bazel, tests/realtikvtest/txntest/txn_test.go
Patch url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/51667.patch
Picked test: type=realtikv path=tests/realtikvtest/txntest/txn_test.go name=TestCheckTxnStatusOnOptimisticTxnBreakConsistency
